title: MTG votes against Antisemitism Awareness Act. Why?
shortDescription: MTG voted against the Antisemitism Awareness act, and cited an age-old trope in doing so.

title: President Biden condemns non-peaceful protests
shortDescription: Amid violent protests surrounding the conflict between Israel and Palestine, President Biden is calling for order and peaceful demonstrations.

title: Biden breaks silence on campus unrest: 'Violence is not protected'
shortDescription: President Biden condemned violence and destruction on college campuses while defending the right for pro-Palestinian protesters to demonstrate.

title: Appeals court won't hear Pa. mail-in ballots case. But will SCOTUS?
shortDescription: Will the nation's highest court take on the Keystone's State election case?

title: Exclusive: Trump won't commit to accepting Wisconsin election results
shortDescription: Trump held out the possibility of contesting the results, telling the Milwaukee Journal Sentinel, 'you have to fight for the right of the country.'

title: Latest from Trump trial blog: Stormy Daniels' attorney takes the stand
shortDescription: Follow live updates as Donald Trump's lawyer Emil Bove cross-examines porn star Stormy Daniels' former lawyer Keith Davidson

title: Biden reprises role as consoler-in-chief
shortDescription: Biden to meet with families of slain officers and more news to start your Thursday.

title: Joe Biden to console families of officers gunned down in Charlotte
shortDescription: President Joe Biden has described the four law-enforcement officers killed in Charlotte as "heroes who made the ultimate sacrifice."

title: Exclusive: Bernie Sanders worries young people underestimate Trump threat
shortDescription: Sen. Bernie Sanders sounds off on the Israel-Hamas war, antisemitism, college protests, and President Joe Biden’s record while in office

title: Democrats to boost abortion ballot measures through state legislatures
shortDescription: Democrats are eyeing key state legislatures in 2024 that they say are crucial to defend abortion access, alongside ballot measures.

title: Arizona Senate votes to repeal abortion ban
shortDescription: The Arizona Senate repealed a 1864 law that would ban almost all abortions. Two Republican state senators voted with Democrats to repeal.

title: Trump warns GOP on abortion, attacks UAW leader: Takeaways from his rallies
shortDescription: During a day off in his hush money trial, Trump spoke about the abortion issue and slammed United Auto Workers President Shawn Fain in Michigan.

title: Biden stays mostly silent amid college campus chaos
shortDescription: President Biden faces a lose-lose proposition with how he responds to Gaza protests on college campuses. So far, he's stayed largely silent.

title: Post misrepresents executive order as election takeover | Fact check
shortDescription: The executive order doesn't mention anything about suspending elections. It revokes executive orders on COVID-19 deemed no longer necessary by Biden.

title: Arizona Senate votes to repeal Civil War-era abortion law
shortDescription: The 1864 law bans abortions in all situations except life-threatening medical emergencies and could impose prison terms for doctors and others who aid in an abortion.

title: Obama blasts Trump for not being considered a 'serious guy' in New York
shortDescription: Former President Barack Obama knocked ex-President Donald Trump in an interview, saying he wasn't considered a “serious guy” in New York.

title: Marjorie Taylor Greene plans to oust Speaker Mike Johnson
shortDescription: Reps. Marjorie Taylor Greene and Thomas Massie announced plans to oust Republican speaker.

title: OnPolitics: Marjorie Taylor Greene moves to oust Mike Johnson
shortDescription: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene, R-Ga., said on Wednesday she's calling up a vote next week to oust House Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La.

title: NH delegates for Biden will count. But what about 2028 primary?
shortDescription: Granite State Democrats shouldn't celebrate: the DNC’s decision doesn't mean New Hampshire will be the first primary officially again in 2028.

title: What's the GOP House majority after NY special elections?
shortDescription: House Speaker Mike Johnson can only spare one Republican on a party-line vote as the GOP majority shrinks to just 217-213.
